metadata.title: States PCS: Meghalaya GK for Meghalaya PSC Exams

questions.question: Which ancient kingdom, known for its Megalithic traditions, ruled parts of the Khasi Hills in present-day Meghalaya?
questions.explanation: The Jaintia Kingdom ruled over parts of the Khasi and Jaintia Hills and was prominent for its Megalithic culture until its annexation by the British in 1835.

questions.question: Which British official was responsible for signing treaties with the Khasi chiefs in the early 19th century?
questions.explanation: David Scott, the British political agent, negotiated and signed treaties with Khasi chiefs, establishing British presence in the Khasi Hills after the Anglo-Burmese War.

questions.question: Which Khasi chief led the revolt against British colonization between 1829 and 1833?
questions.explanation: U Tirot Singh, a Khasi Syiem of Nongkhlaw in present-day Meghalaya, led the Khasi uprising (1829–1833) against the British, resisting road construction plans.

questions.question: Which battle resulted in the defeat and annexation of the Jaintia kingdom by the British?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Raliang in 1835 led to the fall and annexation of the Jaintia kingdom by the British, integrating the Jaintia Hills into British India.

questions.question: Which Christian denomination was the first to introduce formal education in Meghalaya?
questions.explanation: Presbyterian missionaries pioneered formal education in Meghalaya, establishing schools in the Khasi and Jaintia Hills in the 19th century.

questions.question: Which prominent Meghalaya leader was instrumental in founding the All Party Hill Leaders’ Conference (APHLC)?
questions.explanation: Williamson A. Sangma co-founded the APHLC and led efforts that resulted in Meghalaya’s statehood.

questions.question: In which year was Meghalaya officially granted statehood, separating it from Assam?
questions.explanation: Meghalaya became a separate state from Assam on January 21, 1972.

questions.question: Which traditional institution acts as the governing body in many Khasi villages of Meghalaya?
questions.explanation: Dorbar Shnong is the traditional village council in Khasi society, responsible for local governance in many Khasi villages of Meghalaya.

questions.question: Which is the highest peak in Meghalaya?
questions.explanation: Shillong Peak is the tallest point in Meghalaya, standing at 1,965 meters above sea level.

questions.question: Which plateau is the primary geographical feature of Meghalaya?
questions.explanation: The Shillong Plateau is Meghalaya’s main landform, made of ancient rocks and shaping the state’s terrain.
